name: Aaliyah Washington
chief_complaint: I can't breathe, my inhaler isn't helping, please help me
onset_description: Was at a friend's house where there were two cats. Within 30 minutes started wheezing. Used her rescue inhaler 4 times over the past 2 hours with minimal relief. Symptoms rapidly escalating.

severe_dyspnea.description: Can barely get words out between breaths, feels like chest is closing up
severe_dyspnea.character: can't move air in or out, chest feels locked

wheezing.description: Loud whistling sounds with every breath, can hear it without a stethoscope
wheezing.character: diffuse bilateral expiratory wheezing, also some inspiratory wheezing

chest_tightness.description: Feels like someone wrapped a belt around my chest and keeps tightening it

cough.description: Dry, hacking cough that won't stop, makes it even harder to breathe

anxiety.description: I'm really scared, I feel like I'm going to die, this is the worst attack I've ever had
anxiety.character: intense fear, sense of impending doom

diaphoresis.description: Sweating all over even though I'm cold

labs.ABG: pH 7.44, pCO2 33 mmHg, pO2 62 mmHg (hypoxemia with respiratory alkalosis — concerning if pCO2 normalizes or rises, signals fatigue)
labs.BMP: Na 140, K 3.6, Cr 0.7, Glucose 142 (mild stress response), BUN 12
labs.peak_flow: 120 L/min (predicted ~440 L/min, approximately 27% of predicted — severe obstruction)
labs.lactate: 2.4 mmol/L (mildly elevated, reflecting work of breathing)
labs.chest_xray: Hyperinflated lungs, no infiltrates, no pneumothorax, no effusions

past_medical_history: Asthma (diagnosed at age 6, classified as moderate persistent)
2 prior ED visits for asthma exacerbations (ages 14 and 19)
1 prior ICU admission at age 14 for status asthmaticus
medications: Fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair) 250/50mcg inhaled twice daily (admits she's been inconsistent lately)

social_history.alcohol: Occasional social drinking, 1-2 drinks on weekends
social_history.living_situation: Lives in a campus apartment with one roommate. No pets. Carpet in bedroom.
social_history.exercise: Plays intramural volleyball, runs 2-3 times per week (when asthma controlled)

personality: normally outgoing and articulate but currently terrified and struggling to communicate due to respiratory distress, cooperative and eager to do whatever is needed to feel better
communication_style: Speaks in fragmented, short phrases between gasps. Nods or shakes head for yes/no when too winded to talk. Very honest about medication non-compliance when asked. Will text her mom on her phone if she can't verbalize answers. Uses generation-appropriate language.
pain_description_style: Describes the chest tightness vividly ('like my lungs are in a fist') but differentiates it from pain clearly. Knows her asthma well and can tell you this is her worst episode. Rates things quickly on a number scale when asked.

critical_findings: SpO2 89% on room air — significant hypoxemia
Peak flow 27% of predicted — severe obstruction
Tachycardia at 132 bpm reflecting severity and albuterol use
Tachypnea at 34 breaths/min with accessory muscle use
Failed rescue inhaler therapy (4 doses without relief)
History of prior ICU admission for status asthmaticus
pCO2 still low at 33 — if it normalizes, patient is tiring and may need intubation
recommended_interventions: Immediate supplemental oxygen to maintain SpO2 > 93%
Systemic corticosteroids (IV methylprednisolone 125mg or oral prednisone 60mg)
Continuous cardiorespiratory monitoring and pulse oximetry
Serial ABGs — monitor for rising pCO2 (sign of respiratory fatigue)
ICU admission if no improvement within 1-2 hours
Prepare for possible intubation if clinical deterioration
Asthma action plan education and controller medication compliance counseling at discharge
